text:
HEAD AND NECK ONCOLOGY / RADIATION ONCOLOGY
JOINT POST-TREATMENT FOLLOW-UP NOTE

ATTENDING: [Head and Neck Medical Oncology / Radiation Oncology]
VISIT TYPE: Scheduled Post-Treatment Follow-Up (Joint Medical Oncology and Radiation Oncology)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Margaret Osei-Bonsu is a 52-year-old West African-descent female never-smoker presenting for her first scheduled post-treatment clinic visit following completion of definitive concurrent chemoradiation for HPV-positive (p16+) squamous cell carcinoma of the right base of tongue, T2N1M0 by AJCC 8th edition conventional staging (stage I by HPV-positive oropharyngeal staging criteria). She completed her 35th and final fraction of IMRT approximately 10 days ago and presents today for acute toxicity assessment, nutritional status review, and discussion of the post-treatment surveillance plan.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Osei-Bonsu is a 52-year-old music teacher who initially presented with a 3-month history of progressive right-sided odynophagia and a palpable right neck mass. Workup confirmed an HPV-positive (p16 3+, HPV-16 RNA ISH positive) moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the right base of tongue measuring 3.2 cm with a single right level IIA lymph node at 2.1 cm. Staging PET-CT confirmed no distant metastases. NGS identified a PIK3CA p.E545K activating mutation and CDKN2A deletion among other alterations; TMB was low at 6.2 mut/Mb, MSS. After multidisciplinary tumor board review, she was recommended for definitive concurrent chemoradiation rather than upfront surgical resection.

Treatment course: The patient received IMRT using SIB-VMAT technique -- 70 Gy to the high-risk CTV in 35 fractions over 7 weeks, with elective nodal volumes receiving 56-63 Gy per protocol. A PEG tube was placed prophylactically prior to treatment initiation. Concurrent systemic therapy consisted of weekly Platinol at 40 mg/m2; she received 6 of a planned 7 cycles, with cycle 5 held for grade 2 nephrotoxicity (creatinine 1.3 mg/dL, baseline 0.8 mg/dL) and grade 2 thrombocytopenia. Cumulative Platinol delivered was approximately 414 mg/m2, representing approximately 83% of the planned total dose -- felt to be adequate.

The treatment course was complicated by grade 3 oral mucositis beginning in week 4, which required a 3-day inpatient admission for IV hydration, aggressive pain management including morphine PCA transitioning to extended-release oxycodone, and initiation of PEG tube feeds. Since that hospitalization, the patient has been wholly dependent on PEG nutrition (Vital 1.5 at 75 mL/hr x 16 hours/day) with minimal to no oral intake due to severe odynophagia. She also developed grade 2 radiation dermatitis over the right neck (managed with Mepitel film and Aquaphor), grade 2 dysgeusia, grade 2 xerostomia, and grade 1 fatigue. No radiation treatment interruptions occurred. Creatinine has since recovered to approximately 1.0 mg/dL on repeat labs obtained at her last visit.

Today, the patient reports that the mucositis is beginning to improve -- she describes the pain as 4-5/10, down from 9/10 at its worst. She is still not able to tolerate oral liquids comfortably but is hopeful about recovery. She is exhausted, still quite fatigued, and endorses low mood related to the prolonged treatment burden and physical deconditioning. She denies new neck masses, fevers, or changes in her voice quality beyond mild hoarseness that has been present throughout treatment. She is currently taking extended-release oxycodone 20 mg BID with oxycodone 5 mg IR q4h PRN for breakthrough pain, with moderate pain control.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Significant fatigue (patient rates 7/10 on functional scale), 9 kg weight loss total over the course of treatment (from 76 kg pre-treatment to 67 kg today), decreased energy and stamina. No fever or chills.
Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Persistent bilateral xerostomia (markedly reduced saliva since week 3 of radiation), dysgeusia with near-complete loss of taste (everything described as "metallic or like cardboard"), mild bilateral tinnitus (new since Platinol), right-sided odynophagia still present but improving. Mild hoarseness, stable. Trismus not yet formally assessed. No new neck mass. No otalgia.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain or palpitations. Mild lower extremity edema noted during inpatient stay, resolved.
Respiratory: No dyspnea. No cough. No hemoptysis.
Gastrointestinal: PEG tube functioning well. No abdominal pain, nausea currently mild, no vomiting. Constipation managed with polyethylene glycol (PEG tube administered).
Musculoskeletal: No neck stiffness. Mild generalized myalgias from deconditioning.
Neurologic: No peripheral numbness or tingling (no neuropathy).
Psychiatric: Low mood, not meeting criteria for major depression per patient report, but distressed about functional losses. Social work engaged.
Endocrine: No new symptoms; on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily for pre-existing hypothyroidism (TSH not yet re-checked post-radiation).
Skin: Right neck radiation field skin: previously moist desquamation, now in re-epithelialization phase, tender but improving.

---

MEDICATIONS

1. Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (pre-existing hypothyroidism)
2. Oxycodone ER (OxyContin) 20 mg PO BID via PEG tube
3. Oxycodone IR 5 mg PO/PEG q4h PRN breakthrough pain
4. Ondansetron 4 mg PO q8h PRN nausea
5. Polyethylene glycol 17 g PO daily via PEG (constipation)
6. Vital 1.5 tube feed formula at 75 mL/hr x 16 hours/day via PEG
7. Magic mouthwash (diphenhydramine 12.5 mg/lidocaine 2%/Maalox) swish-and-spit q4h PRN mucositis
8. Dexamethasone 0.1% oral rinse swish-and-spit TID (mucositis)
9. Aquaphor ointment topically to right neck skin TID
10. Proton pump inhibitor (omeprazole 20 mg daily via PEG)

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Hypothyroidism (well-controlled pre-treatment on levothyroxine 75 mcg)
- HPV-positive right base of tongue SCC, T2N1M0 (stage I HPV-OPC staging) -- current diagnosis
- Grade 2 nephrotoxicity during cis treatment (resolved)

SOCIAL HISTORY: High school music teacher, currently on medical leave. Never-smoker. No alcohol use. West African descent. Monogamous relationship. Lives with partner. Supportive home environment; partner has been primary caregiver during treatment.

FAMILY HISTORY: No family history of head and neck cancers. No family history of BRCA-related cancers known; BRCA2 VUS (p.D2723H) identified on somatic NGS, germline testing pending.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

General: The patient appears tired and mildly cachectic, though more alert than at her last visit during the inpatient admission. She is in no acute distress. She is speaking in full sentences, though with a mild change in voice quality (slightly muffled/hypernasalized).

Vital Signs: Blood pressure 108/70 mmHg, heart rate 78 bpm, temperature 37.0 C, SpO2 98% on room air, weight 67 kg (down 9 kg from baseline of 76 kg).

Head and Neck:
- Oral cavity/oropharynx: Mucositis clearly improving compared to the height of treatment. Residual grade 1-2 patchy pseudomembranous changes visible at the base of tongue and bilateral soft palate; no longer the confluent grade 3 changes seen during admission. The right base of tongue primary site is difficult to fully visualize on mirror exam but the posterior oropharynx appears less edematous. Significant erythema throughout the irradiated oropharyngeal mucosa.
- Neck: Right level II lymph node previously measuring 2.1 cm and palpable at diagnosis -- today no longer palpable. The right anterior neck skin in the radiation field shows patchy re-epithelialization with residual erythema and superficial desquamation in the process of healing; no frank moist desquamation at this visit. No left-sided adenopathy. No palpable submandibular masses. Bilateral neck supple.
- Salivary glands: Parotid region non-tender bilaterally. Submandibular glands non-enlarged.
- Ears: External canals clear bilaterally. Tympanic membranes intact.

Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Tongue midline on protrusion, no tongue deviation or atrophy. No shoulder droop (CN XI intact). Mild generalized weakness consistent with deconditioning.

Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No stridor.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs.

Abdomen: PEG site: clean, granulation tissue forming, no infection or leakage. Abdomen soft, non-tender.

Extremities: No peripheral edema. No cyanosis.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (from labs drawn today)

- CBC: WBC 5.4 x10^9/L (normal), Hgb 10.8 g/dL (mild anemia, down from 13.2 pre-treatment -- consistent with chemoradiation-associated anemia), platelets 198 x10^9/L (recovered from prior nadirs)
- BMP: Sodium 138, Potassium 3.8, Chloride 102, Bicarbonate 26, BUN 14, Creatinine 1.0 mg/dL (improved from 1.3 at nadir; eGFR ~62 mL/min, mildly reduced from baseline >90, still trending toward recovery)
- Magnesium 2.0 mg/dL (low-normal; monitoring given prior Platinol-associated hypomagnesemia)
- Phosphorus 3.3 mg/dL (normal)
- Albumin 3.1 g/dL (low; consistent with nutritional deficits and inflammation)
- Prealbumin 14 mg/dL (low; nutritional depletion)
- TSH: 5.2 mIU/L (elevated, above pre-treatment baseline; raising concern for radiation-induced hypothyroidism in addition to pre-existing primary hypothyroidism -- levothyroxine dose adjustment to be considered)
- LFTs: AST, ALT, alkaline phosphatase within normal limits
- Coagulation: INR 1.0

---

IMAGING

No restaging imaging has been performed at this visit -- per standard practice and NCCN guidelines for HPV-positive oropharyngeal SCC, restaging PET-CT and MRI of the neck are planned at 8-12 weeks post-completion of chemoradiation to allow sufficient time for inflammatory changes to resolve and to maximize sensitivity/specificity for detecting residual disease. Imaging will be ordered and scheduled for approximately 8-10 weeks from today.

Noted on clinical examination: The previously palpable right level II lymph node is no longer palpable. This is an encouraging early clinical indicator but is not sufficient to confirm response in lieu of formal restaging imaging.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Osei-Bonsu is a 52-year-old never-smoker with HPV-positive (p16+, HPV-16) right base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma, T2N1M0 (stage I HPV-OPC staging), who has just completed her definitive concurrent chemoradiation course -- 70 Gy IMRT with 6 of 7 planned weekly Platinol cycles (cumulative ~414 mg/m2, approximately 83% planned dose). She is seen here approximately 10 days after her final radiation fraction.

1. TREATMENT COMPLETION AND EARLY RESPONSE ASSESSMENT:
The patient has completed definitive treatment. Clinically, the previously palpable right level IIA node is no longer palpable, which is an encouraging early sign. Formal restaging with PET-CT and MRI of the neck will be scheduled for 8-10 weeks post-treatment completion per standard practice. We will review those results together. The patient was counseled that it is too early at this visit to make a definitive statement about tumor response, and that she should not interpret the improving symptoms alone as confirmed remission. She understands.

2. MUCOSITIS (GRADE 1-2, IMPROVING FROM GRADE 3 NADIR):
Mucositis is resolving as expected in the weeks following radiation completion. The peak was grade 3, requiring hospitalization in week 4 -- this has now improved to grade 1-2. Continue magic mouthwash and dexamethasone oral rinse for comfort. Continue oxycodone ER for background pain management; will plan opioid dose taper beginning in 2-3 weeks as mucositis continues to resolve -- will start by decreasing IR breakthrough frequency, then taper ER dose. Counseled on bowel regimen given opioid use. Continue PPI.

3. NUTRITIONAL STATUS AND PEG TUBE:
The patient has lost 9 kg (12% of pre-treatment body weight). She remains fully PEG-dependent at this time. Albumin and prealbumin are low, consistent with significant nutritional depletion. Will continue current PEG tube feeds (Vital 1.5 at goal rate) and involve dietetics for formal reassessment. Target caloric intake remains ~2200 kcal/day. The patient was counseled that PEG removal is not appropriate at this time and that oral intake should be reintroduced gradually as mucositis resolves and swallowing function recovers. She expressed strong motivation to regain the ability to eat normally -- this is an important quality-of-life priority.

4. SWALLOWING REHABILITATION:
Speech-language pathology to begin formal swallowing rehabilitation this week. This will include: thermal-tactile stimulation exercises, effortful swallowing exercises, Mendelsohn maneuver training, and supraglottic swallow techniques. The goal is to prevent post-radiation dysphagia from worsening due to fibrosis and to begin the process of oral diet reintroduction. A formal FEES (functional endoscopic evaluation of swallowing) will be scheduled at 4-6 weeks post-treatment to assess swallowing mechanics and guide diet advancement.

5. RADIATION DERMATITIS (HEALING, RIGHT NECK):
The right neck skin is in the re-epithelialization phase, improving from grade 2 moist desquamation. Continue Aquaphor topically TID. Avoid sun exposure and occlusive dressings. No antibiotics required at this time. Skin should fully heal within 3-4 weeks.

6. NEPHROTOXICITY (RECOVERING):
Creatinine now 1.0 mg/dL, down from nadir of 1.3 mg/dL. eGFR approximately 62 mL/min, still below pre-treatment baseline of >90 mL/min. No further cis is planned. Will monitor creatinine at next visit. Patient to maintain adequate oral hydration as she transitions off PEG. Given mildly depressed eGFR, avoid NSAIDs (patient counseled). Low threshold to recheck renal function if patient becomes symptomatic.

7. HYPOMAGNESEMIA (BORDERLINE) AND ANEMIA:
Magnesium 2.0 mg/dL -- borderline low. Will supplement with magnesium oxide 400 mg PO BID via PEG until dietary intake recovers. Mild normocytic anemia (Hgb 10.8) consistent with chemoradiation effect; no indication for erythropoietin or transfusion at this level. Expect gradual recovery over 3-6 months. Recheck CBC at next visit.

8. HYPOTHYROIDISM (TSH ELEVATED):
TSH now 5.2 mIU/L, elevated above her pre-treatment well-controlled baseline on levothyroxine 75 mcg. This is consistent with radiation-induced direct thyroid gland injury (the thyroid gland sits partially within the lower neck irradiation fields) superimposed on pre-existing primary hypothyroidism. Increasing levothyroxine from 75 mcg to 100 mcg daily. Will recheck TSH in 6-8 weeks. Patient counseled about signs/symptoms of hypothyroidism (fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation) and to contact us if she notices worsening. She will need ongoing thyroid function monitoring and may require further levothyroxine adjustments; thyroid ultrasound will be incorporated into long-term surveillance.

9. TINNITUS / OTOTOXICITY MONITORING:
Mild bilateral tinnitus noted by patient since Platinol treatment. Monitoring audiogram was performed after the 4th Platinol cycle and was stable per prior records. A formal audiogram will be repeated at approximately 3 months post-treatment. Patient counseled on Platinol-associated sensorineural hearing loss and the importance of hearing protection going forward.

10. PSYCHOSOCIAL:
Patient appears emotionally strained by the cumulative toll of treatment. Low mood, significant functional losses (dietary, vocal), and prolonged medical leave from her work as a music teacher (which she clearly values greatly). Social work is actively following. She was reassured that her clinical trajectory appears favorable -- completing treatment without interruption and with adequate cis delivery is a good prognostic indicator in HPV-positive OPSCC. We discussed the expected timeline for recovery of swallowing, taste, and energy over the coming 3-6 months. She was encouraged and expressed optimism about returning to work.

11. GERMLINE GENETICS:
Patient was reminded about the BRCA2 VUS (p.D2723H, AF 48% on somatic NGS, likely germline) identified at the time of tumor NGS. Referral to cancer genetics was placed at the time of tumor board, and she was encouraged to follow up with genetics counseling once she has regained strength after treatment. This does not change current management but is important for long-term risk stratification.

12. SURVEILLANCE PLANNING:
Formal restaging imaging will be ordered now:
- PET-CT (18F-FDG) at approximately 10 weeks post-treatment completion
- MRI neck with and without contrast at approximately 10 weeks post-treatment (concurrent with PET-CT)
Follow-up visit after imaging review will be scheduled with both medical oncology and radiation oncology. Per NCCN HPV-positive OPSCC surveillance guidelines, clinic visits are recommended every 1-3 months in year 1, every 2-6 months in year 2, and annually thereafter beyond year 5. Annual thyroid function testing is mandatory given prior neck irradiation.

Return to clinic: 2 weeks for toxicity check-in and labs, then after restaging imaging is completed at approximately 10 weeks post-treatment.

ECOG Performance Status: 2 (limited self-care ability, in bed or chair less than 50% of waking hours -- primarily due to fatigue, pain, and inability to eat orally)

Cancer Status: Indeterminate -- too early post-treatment to assess response. Restaging imaging pending.

Time spent with patient: 55 minutes. Greater than 50% of this time was spent counseling and coordinating care.

[Electronically signed by Attending Physician, Head and Neck Medical Oncology]
[Co-signed by Attending Physician, Radiation Oncology]
